source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Estimation/BayesianAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- exact oracle values dumped from the REAL C# BayesianAnalysis (DEMCzs sampler) via tools/oracle_emitter (Task T12), run with parallel:false so chain generation is serial (matching the C++ port). TOLERANCES: (1) chain_value at rel 1e-11 -- DEMCzs is pure-RNG (Mersenne Twister + differential-evolution + snooker proposals), no gradients; the seeded MarkovChains reproduce bit-identically across C#/C++/R/Python at the Phase-3 DEMCzs digest tier (see fixtures/sampling/mcmc/demczs.json's normal_short_exact; the first-iteration values 25229.185.../38800.788... are the SAME initial population, confirming the shared seed cascade). One entry (chain 3, draw 0, mu) froze on its prior's lower bound in both languages -> mode 'equal' (rel against exact 0 is undefined). (2) posterior_mean at rel 1e-9, and (3) dic/waic/looic at rel 1e-8 (waic/looic) and rel 1e-6 (dic). These read the pooled Output samples, which for a POPULATION sampler (DEMCz/DEMCzs) drift cross-language once the OutputLength-driven output phase runs long enough for sub-ULP accept/reject noise to amplify (see fixtures/README.md's population-sampler divergence finding). This case deliberately sets output_length=400 (output phase = ceil(400/4)=100 iterations, matching the 100-iteration MarkovChains window and staying BEFORE that amplification), so the Output-derived quantities reproduce to ~1e-11..1e-14 rather than needing the 0.05 output-phase tolerance the long-run rstan cases use. dic keeps rel 1e-6 headroom for its Parallel.For (Tools.ParallelAdd) reduction, which is not bit-reproducible even C#-to-C# (measured ~1e-13 absolute run-to-run); NOT faked. NOTE (port-fidelity): the C# univariate model applies a Jeffreys 1/scale prior by default (UseJeffreysRuleForScale=true); the C++ core's UnivariateDistributionModel::prior_log_likelihood was extended in T12 to match, which is why MAP and these Bayesian posteriors reproduce. Validate() bounds (chains 4-20, warmup>=50, iterations>=100, output_length>=100) are respected.
